topic: nan's church fundraiser — she volunteered him

turns.content: bruv my nan has done it again 😂 she's volunteered me for something without asking
Ha, what's she signed you up for this time?

her church is doing a fundraiser right and apparently she told the pastor that her grandson will come and help set up and run a stall and do whatever else needs doing. i found out when she called me yesterday like oh Marcus the fundraiser is saturday make sure you wear something nice
Classic grandmother move -- informing you of your plans rather than asking. What kind of fundraiser is it, and what does she actually need you to do?

its like a summer fair type thing in the church hall. cake sale food stalls raffle tombola all that. she wants me to help set up tables in the morning then run one of the food stalls during the day. which is fine i dont mind helping but she could have asked me first init 😂 i had plans to watch the football with my boys
To be fair, in the world of grandmothers and church fundraisers, your football plans never stood a chance. Are you going to tell her you had other plans or just go with it?

man you cant say no to my nan thats not how it works. she does this little thing where she goes oh its fine dont worry about it ill manage on my own and then you feel like the worst person alive. nah im going. the football can wait
The guilt trip -- a timeless and devastatingly effective technique. At least you'll probably get well fed out of it. What's the food stall situation?

see thats the upside tbh. the church ladies go MAD with the cooking. im talking jerk chicken curry goat rice and peas macaroni pie plantain the lot. and they always make too much so i know im coming home with bare containers of food. might actually sort my meals for the whole week if im lucky
So really she's doing you a favour -- free food for the week in exchange for a few hours of moving tables. That's a solid deal. Do you know what you're actually selling on your stall?

she said drinks and snacks stall so probably just serving juice and patties and stuff. easy enough. the hard part is going to be all the aunties asking me if i have a girlfriend yet because that happens every single time i go to anything church related. its like a sport for them

You're going to need a game plan for that. Have a few deflection lines ready -- redirect to your studies, ask them about their own grandkids, that sort of thing. Or just accept your fate and let them introduce you to every eligible granddaughter in the congregation.

looool nah im going with the studies deflection for sure. oh im too busy with uni right now you know how it is. works every time because then they start telling me how proud they are and how good it is that young people are studying and i escape the girlfriend interrogation. ive got it down to an art at this point

Sounds like you've been through this enough times to have a strategy. Honestly though, it sounds like it'll be a good day despite the early morning setup. Your nan clearly loves having you there, the food situation is a major win, and a few hours helping out isn't the worst way to spend a Saturday.

yeah ngl when i stop complaining about it i actually dont mind. my nan loves it when im there and all her friends hype me up about uni and stuff. its nice you know. alright let me go find something nice to wear before she rings me again to remind me 😂 cheers
Enjoy it. And make sure you secure those leftover containers before anyone else gets to them. Good luck dodging the aunties.
